page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor operating at 3.0 GHz, delivering 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB per core.

This instance integrates dedicated hardware acceleration, featuring four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with a combined VRAM of 32 GB. Local storage is bundled as 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific workloads, including ray tracing, PassMark CPU tests, and database operations. Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong throughput. However, single-core CPU performance is relatively poor, placing in the bottom 10% of stress-ng benchmarks, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak, reflecting architectural limitations for massive model inference.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4ad.16xlarge offers a balanced profile for graphics-heavy workloads and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage and multi-GPU acceleration without the premium of high-end enterprise AI chips. It is highly suited for graphics rendering, ray tracing, database hosting, and moderate machine learning tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a graphics-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 32 GB of total VRAM and includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in ray tracing, PassMark CPU tests, and database operations, though single-core CPU performance and large-scale LLM text generation are weaker. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient solution for multi-GPU graphics rendering, database hosting, and parallel computing tasks that leverage local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD local storage.
tagline: A multi-GPU instance with local NVMe storage, optimized for graphics rendering, ray tracing, and high-throughput database operations.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 at 3.0 GHz
Four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong performance in ray tracing and database operations
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
